items.title: FPL 2026/27 IS HERE! | MY TEAM for Fantasy Premier League New Season Gameweek 1
items.headline: Bruno Fernandes and Erling Haaland anchor a Gameweek 4 wildcard draft
items.summary:
The opening FPL 2026/27 draft is built around an aggressive early wildcard plan, most likely in Gameweek 4. The logic is that the first three weeks offer unusually clear short-term fixture runs, while also giving managers time to learn which players are affected by post-World Cup rest, new systems and the new defensive contribution points. Manchester United’s first three fixtures — Hull, Ipswich and Everton — drive several picks, while Liverpool’s early run and the Gameweek 3/4 fixture swings for Arsenal, Chelsea and United make a Gameweek 4 reset look attractive.

That short-term strategy justifies spending £5.0m on Lammens in goal rather than settling for a £4.5m keeper. United’s fixtures are strong enough to play him every week before potentially downgrading on wildcard. The defence leans on Virgil van Dijk as a premium Liverpool route, with clean-sheet potential, aerial threat and defensive contribution points making him a possible “this season’s Gabriel” at £6.5m. Arsenal’s Mosquera is preferred as a cheaper £5.5m way into their defence while William Saliba is out for several months, although Gabriel and Raya remain stronger but more expensive options. Neco Williams is selected for his attacking wing-back role under Oliver Glasner at Nottingham Forest, with Coventry’s Van Ewijk on the bench specifically to cover Williams’ tough Gameweek 2 trip to Liverpool.

Midfield is shaped by a Manchester United double-up of Bruno Fernandes and Bryan Mbeumo. Bruno is a leading captaincy candidate for Gameweeks 1 and 2 against Hull and Ipswich, while Mbeumo is backed because he has a clear route to minutes on the right, faces excellent early fixtures and has had a fresh summer. Dominik Szoboszlai is included as a Liverpool attacker with set-piece upside in the absence of Mohamed Salah and possible penalty involvement, while Anton Stach is the “glue” pick: a reliable £6.0m midfielder with minutes, set pieces and defensive contribution potential rather than a high-risk cheap punt.

Up front, Erling Haaland is treated as close to essential because Manchester City face Bournemouth, Crystal Palace and Coventry in the first three gameweeks, making him the standout Gameweek 3 captain and a viable armband option throughout. João Pedro is the second striker, valued at £7.5m after a consistent previous season and preferred over less proven or streakier forwards such as Thiago, Calvert-Lewin, Welbeck, Solanke, Isak and Watkins. The bench is purely structural: Phillips is the £4.0m non-playing goalkeeper, Gomez is the preferred initial £5.0m midfielder thanks to set pieces and a useful Gameweek 3 fixture, Van Ewijk is the rotating £4.0m defender, and Walle Egeli is the best-looking £4.5m forward because he may at least get occasional Ipswich minutes.
key_points: A Gameweek 4 wildcard is the planned structure, allowing short-term fixture attacks and time to assess World Cup fatigue, rotations and defensive contribution trends.
Bruno Fernandes is the current Gameweek 1 captain choice away to Hull, with Erling Haaland vice-captain; those two dominate the captaincy discussion for the first three weeks.
Manchester United are heavily targeted early: Lammens in goal plus Bruno Fernandes and Bryan Mbeumo in midfield because they face Hull, Ipswich and Everton before fixtures stiffen.
Virgil van Dijk is preferred as the Liverpool defensive pick at £6.5m, with Van Dijk’s aerial threat and defensive contribution potential making him more appealing than cheaper alternatives for now.
Mosquera offers a £5.5m Arsenal defensive route while Saliba is injured, saving £2.5m compared with Gabriel despite reduced attacking threat.
Neco Williams is backed as a high-upside Nottingham Forest wing-back under Oliver Glasner, but Van Ewijk is picked on the bench to cover Williams’ Liverpool away fixture in Gameweek 2.
Dominik Szoboszlai gains appeal without Mohamed Salah because he may take more set pieces and could be involved in penalties for Liverpool.
João Pedro is selected as the £7.5m second forward, but Chelsea investment may be increased from Gameweek 4 onwards on wildcard.

items.title: My First Draft 2026/27 #fpl #fantasypremierleague #fpltips
items.headline: Haaland anchors a budget-heavy first FPL draft built around Palmer, cheap defence and 6.0m forwards
items.summary: Up front, the draft is built around Erling Haaland despite his huge 15.5m price tag, with the view that he is still very difficult to go without. The squad is then completed by taking advantage of a strong group of forwards around 6.0m, allowing premium money to stay concentrated in the biggest captaincy and attacking upside picks.
key_points: Kinsky is the 4.5m goalkeeper pick, with funds saved in goal for use further forward.
Defence is budget-focused: Rodon at 4.5m, Neco Williams as an attacking-value punt, and Mosca at 5.5m if Arsenal do not add another right centre-back.
Fernandez is close to essential in the draft because of Hull away in Gameweek 1 and Ipswich at home in Gameweek 2.
Cole Palmer at 9.5m is the most likely downgrade, but a summer rest and Alonso’s arrival keep him in contention.
Liverpool penalties are a key watch with Salah gone: Ekitike is the leading guess, with Szoboszlai also possible.
Sarr is backed to do well despite a managerial change.
Ndiaye appeals as a cheaper Everton penalty taker compared with João Pedro.
Haaland stays in the team even at 15.5m, supported by value forwards around the 6.0m bracket.

items.title: MY FIRST FPL 2026/27 TEAM 🔥 BEST RANK 3K! 🌍
items.summary: {"headline":"Triple Chelsea attack and £15.5m Haaland headline an aggressive first FPL draft","summary":"The first draft leans into the new 2026/27 FPL landscape, where official price changes now start after Gameweek 1, live rank and live league data are built into the game, and bonus-point tweaks appear to help goalkeepers, full-backs and attacking players. The structure is a 3-5-2 with money pushed into midfield, reflecting the view that the best value and highest point potential sit there after attacking midfielders are no longer penalised in BPS for being tackled.","In goal, Verbruggen is preferred at £4.5m because Brighton rank fifth for projected clean sheets over the opening six gameweeks, behind Arsenal, Manchester City, Manchester United and Everton, while premium options such as £6.0m Raya feel expensive. The defence is Guehi, Jac and Mosquera: Guehi offers Arsenal clean-sheet upside and attacking threat at £6.0m, though World Cup rest rules may put some finalists at risk for Gameweek 1; Mosquera is a £5.5m route into Arsenal’s back line while Saliba is sidelined, provided Arsenal do not sign another right-sided centre-back; and Jac is backed as a potentially underpriced £5.0m Liverpool starter if Arne Slot’s side tighten up after his move from Bournemouth.","Midfield is built around Mbeumo, Rogers, Palmer, Szoboszlai and Anderson. Mbeumo is chosen over Bruno Fernandes despite Manchester United’s excellent Gameweek 1 trip to Hull, largely because he is £4.0m cheaper and posted a much stronger expected goal involvement than Cunha last season. Szoboszlai stands out at £7.0m thanks to multiple routes to points: set pieces, possible penalties, corners, free kicks and defensive contributions. Anderson, now at Manchester City, is seen as a steady points source after leading the league for defensive contribution returns last season, even if his role changes from his Forest days.","The biggest swing is a triple Chelsea attack of Palmer, Rogers and João Pedro alongside Haaland up front. Chelsea have no Europe, a stronger squad and Xabi Alonso in charge, making them a possible version of last season’s high-performing Manchester United setup. The upside is especially clear in Gameweek 4, when Chelsea host Hull and Palmer could be captained, effectively doubling down on that attack. The risk is obvious: Palmer and Rogers both had uneven seasons, and triple attack is more volatile than a balanced template. Haaland at £15.5m remains in despite the huge price, partly because there are fewer premium alternatives with Salah gone, Saka set to miss the start and Palmer still carrying some uncertainty.","key_points":["Initial formation is 3-5-2, with Verbruggen in goal and no reliance on ultra-cheap midfielders.","Mbeumo is preferred to Bruno Fernandes because he is £4.0m cheaper and offers strong underlying numbers, despite United’s Gameweek 1 fixture away to Hull.","Triple Chelsea attack is the main differential: Palmer, Rogers and João Pedro are backed due to no European football, Xabi Alonso’s arrival and a strong Gameweek 4 home fixture against Hull.","Haaland stays in at £15.5m because premium alternatives are limited, though the price forces sacrifices elsewhere.","Szoboszlai at £7.0m is highlighted as underpriced thanks to set pieces, possible penalties and defensive contribution potential.","Mosquera is a cheap Arsenal defensive route at £5.5m while Saliba is out, but his security depends on Arsenal’s transfer activity.","World Cup finalists may miss or be eased into Gameweek 1 because of the mandatory three-week rest period, affecting early team selection.","Bonus-point changes should help goalkeepers, full-backs and attackers, while defensive contributions make players such as Anderson more attractive."]}

The squad also keeps £0.5m in the bank to react quickly to early price moves and unexpected transfer-window developments.

Rushworth, Tyreek Mitchell, Grimes and Giles are on the bench. In goal, Kinsky is paired with Rushworth in the hope Rushworth completes a move back to Coventry, where his save volume and clean-sheet record in the Championship make him a playable rotator. If that move fails, a switch to a Manchester United goalkeeper such as Lammens, plus a £4.0m backup, is under consideration.

Defence is built on value rather than premiums. Gabriel is admired but too restrictive at his price, while an Arsenal centre-back at £4.5m — if transfer rumours produce one — could become close to essential. Van Hecke offers a cheaper route into Spurs than Pedro Porro, with set-piece threat and possible defensive-contribution upside, though doubling on a Spurs defence remains a risk. Jaka is preferred as a budget route into Liverpool’s defence, provided pre-season confirms his fitness after a shoulder injury and his starting role alongside Virgil van Dijk; Kerkez is the alternative with more attacking appeal but a higher price and possible Tsimikas competition.

items.title: FPL 2026-27 IS GOING LIVE! | Fantasy Premier League Launch Party
MY FPL FIRST DRAFT! 📱 FPL IS LIVE 🚨 | Fantasy Premier League Tips 2026/27
items.headline: Haaland and Bruno make first 26-27 FPL draft as £8m Gabriel is swerved
items.summary:
The first draft for the 2026-27 FPL season is built around Erling Haaland and Bruno Fernandes, but not the full premium trio: Gabriel is left out at £8.0m. The view is that FPL has priced the headline names fairly, while leaving enough value elsewhere to build a strong squad. New game changes are mostly quality-of-life upgrades rather than a major rules shake-up: predicted price-change trends are now built into the site, price changes move to midnight UK time, ranks and mini-leagues update live, and gameweeks remain open until 9am the morning after the final match so late bonus and Defcon adjustments count properly.

The squad starts with David Raya in goal and a £4.0m backup, with Arsenal defensive cover still seen as important despite a mixed opening run after Coventry at home in Gameweek 1. Gabriel is considered too expensive, so the cheaper Arsenal defenders are the focus: Jurrien Timber at £6.5m, William Saliba at £6.0m when fit, and especially the £5.5m group of Mosquera, Riccardo Calafiori and Piero Hincapie if one looks nailed before the deadline. The defence also includes Harry Maguire at £5.0m for minutes, set-piece threat and Defcon potential, Nottingham Forest’s £4.5m Reyna Cunha under Oliver Glasner, Coventry’s £4.0m Thomas as an attacking bench option, and Malick Tchou, whose fixtures improve after a tough Liverpool opener.

In midfield, Bruno Fernandes is the first pick because of Manchester United’s early fixtures, though there is an acknowledgement that he could become a cheaper midfielder if he is not a convincing Gameweek 1 captain. Morgan Rogers at £7.5m is backed to play in a Chelsea attacking role behind Joao Pedro alongside Cole Palmer, while Dominik Szoboszlai is chosen over the slightly more expensive Wirtz due to budget, with possible penalties and set pieces adding appeal. James Garner is the value pick from Everton’s good opening schedule, profiled as a steady Defcon-style option in the mould of last season’s Elliot Anderson, with a £4.5m midfielder completing the bench.

The structure is currently 3-4-3 because the forward pricing looks generous. Haaland is locked in, Joao Pedro is considered excellent value at £7.5m given Chelsea’s early fixtures, and Dominic Calvert-Lewin takes the final forward slot at £6.0m ahead of alternatives such as Igor Jesus and Osula. Chip strategy is deliberately flexible: there is no obvious early wildcard window yet, Free Hit is only loosely pencilled around Gameweek 4 or Gameweek 12, Triple Captain is likely to target Haaland’s home games against newly promoted Coventry, Ipswich or Hull, and Bench Boost is more likely after a wildcard despite some early fixture-based options.
key_points: First draft keeps Erling Haaland and Bruno Fernandes but avoids £8.0m Gabriel, using cheaper Arsenal defensive routes instead.
Current squad structure is 3-4-3: Raya; Arsenal defender, Maguire, Reyna Cunha; Bruno, Rogers, Szoboszlai, Garner; Haaland, Joao Pedro, Calvert-Lewin.
Arsenal’s defence is still targeted despite tricky fixtures after Coventry in Gameweek 1, with Mosquera, Calafiori or Hincapie at £5.5m key if one becomes nailed.
Joao Pedro at £7.5m and Morgan Rogers at £7.5m form a Chelsea double-up, with good early fixtures but some risk of over-investing in one attack.
Calvert-Lewin is the preferred £6.0m forward for now, though Igor Jesus and Osula are live alternatives to monitor in pre-season.
No fixed early wildcard is planned; the aim is to save it until the squad genuinely needs a reset.
Free Hit is on the watchlist for Gameweek 4, when Manchester United play Manchester City and Chelsea face Hull, or Gameweek 12, when Arsenal play Manchester City.
Triple Captain options centre on Haaland’s first-half home fixtures against newly promoted Coventry, Ipswich and Hull.

items.title: MY FIRST FPL DRAFT 🔥 588 IN THE 🌏 LAST SEASON 🚀  FANTASY PREMIER LEAGUE 26/27 TIPS
items.headline: Haaland and Fernandes anchor first 2026/27 FPL draft despite cheap-defence trade-off
items.summary:
The first 2026/27 FPL draft is built around Erling Haaland at £15.5m and Bruno Fernandes at £12.0m, with the view that captaincy security outweighs the money saved by going without the Manchester City striker. Haaland’s early run — Bournemouth at home in Gameweek 1, Palace away in Gameweek 2, Coventry at home in Gameweek 3, Sunderland at home in Gameweek 5 and Ipswich at home in Gameweek 7 — makes him difficult to fade, even with Fernandes offering strong alternatives away to Hull and at home to Ipswich in the opening two weeks.

The squad starts with Kinsky in goal at £4.5m, preferred as a set-and-forget Spurs route after signs of defensive improvement under De Zerbi, with a £4.0m non-playing goalkeeper alongside him unless a starter emerges. Defence is deliberately inexpensive for now: Mosquera at £5.5m is the Arsenal value pick while Saliba struggles for fitness, though his minutes may come under threat if Arsenal sign another centre-back. Nico Williams of Nottingham Forest, Leeds’ Rodon, Fulham’s Robinson and Newcastle’s Lewis Hall complete the back line, with Robinson particularly interesting as a possible £4.5m penalty-taking defender after Fulham’s departures.

Midfield is a five-man unit for the initial 3-5-2. Fernandes is close to locked because of his minutes, penalties and early fixtures, while Cole Palmer at £9.5m is backed to outperform that price if Chelsea improve under Alonso and he remains nailed on penalties. Szoboszlai at £7.0m is included as a potentially underpriced Liverpool midfielder if he is nailed and in the penalty conversation after Salah’s exit, though Isak may still be the likelier taker. Sarr at £6.5m and Ndiaye at £6.0m add reliable minutes and goal threat, with Ndiaye preferred to more expensive alternatives because his underlying numbers and penalty role look strong.

Up front, Haaland is joined by Calvert-Lewin at £6.0m and a £4.5m bench forward, but there is a clear route toward 3-4-3. Several £6.0m forwards look viable if preseason confirms their minutes: Calvert-Lewin at Leeds, Evanilson, Newcastle’s Osula, Igor Jesus under Glasner and Solanke at Spurs if he stays fit and keeps penalties. The main unresolved question is whether to drop Sarr or Ndiaye to fund a third forward, while Palmer is the likeliest premium midfielder to downgrade if extra money is needed elsewhere.
key_points: Haaland is the preferred captaincy anchor despite his £15.5m price, with Bournemouth, Coventry, Sunderland and Ipswich among City’s first seven fixtures.
Bruno Fernandes is also close to essential early on, with Hull away in Gameweek 1 and Ipswich at home in Gameweek 2, plus penalties and reliable minutes.
Kinsky is the £4.5m goalkeeper pick, offering cheap Spurs defensive coverage and a set-and-forget structure.
Defence is budget-led: Mosquera, Nico Williams, Rodon, Robinson and Lewis Hall are selected, with Gabriel at £8.0m considered too expensive for now.
Mosquera is a strong short-term Arsenal value pick if Saliba misses the start, but his minutes carry risk if Saliba returns quickly or Arsenal sign another centre-back.
Szoboszlai at £7.0m is included for Liverpool value and possible penalties, though Isak may be the more likely taker.
A move from 3-5-2 to 3-4-3 is likely to be tested because several £6.0m forwards — including Calvert-Lewin, Evanilson, Osula, Igor Jesus and Solanke — look appealing.
Palmer is liked at £9.5m, but he is the premium midfielder most likely to be downgraded to free funds for defence or a third forward.

items.title: FPL IS LIVE - LAUNCH PARTY 🚨
items.headline: Haaland’s £15.5m price tag makes Bruno, Gabriel and Isak the key early FPL calls
items.summary:
FPL is live for 2026/27 and the first reaction is that squad building is much tighter than usual. Erling Haaland comes in as the most expensive player in FPL history at £15.5m, Bruno Fernandes rises to £12.0m after a huge assist season, and Gabriel is priced at £8.0m after breaking 200 points. The early conclusion is that fitting all three is too damaging to the rest of the squad; the preferred structure is Haaland plus one premium midfielder, with Gabriel likely sacrificed despite Arsenal’s strong defensive projections.

Haaland is the initial captaincy favourite for Gameweek 1 at home to Bournemouth, but Bruno is a genuine alternative or vice-captain with Manchester United opening against Hull and then Ipswich in Gameweek 2. Bruno’s price is high, and United now have Europe to manage, but the fixtures are strong enough to keep him firmly in the conversation. Chelsea assets are also a major focus because they have no European football, a strong squad and Xabi Alonso in charge, with Cole Palmer at £9.5m and Morgan Rogers at around £7.0m-£7.5m both looking important depending on roles.

There is value up front if the budget squeeze becomes too severe. Alexander Isak at £9.0m stands out as a strong route into Liverpool’s attack, especially if he stays fit and has penalties, while Igor Thiago at £8.0m is tempting for Brentford but carries the risk that last season’s penalty-heavy output is not repeated. Justin Kluivert’s move from forward to midfielder at £7.5m is noted, but Bournemouth’s opening run — Manchester City, Everton, Newcastle, Brentford, Liverpool and Chelsea in the first six — makes him more of a wait-and-see despite penalty appeal. Phil Foden at £7.0m is acknowledged as a future purple-patch pick but remains hard to trust for starts.

The rule changes shape the first draft as much as the prices. Full-backs gain from the bonus-point tweaks, which boosts players such as Milos Kerkez and Pedro Porro, while midfielders may benefit from the removal of negative BPS for being tackled. Defensive contribution points remain, but they do not scale beyond the threshold. Arsenal attackers are treated cautiously because of injuries and rotation concerns, with Bukayo Saka expected to miss the start, Declan Rice uncertain and the left-wing situation unclear. The first draft leans toward a Haaland-led structure, a premium midfielder, Rogers, a value forward, cheaper defenders and a likely 3-5-2 or flexible setup rather than spending heavily on Gabriel.
key_points: Erling Haaland is £15.5m and starts as the preferred Gameweek 1 captain at home to Bournemouth.
Bruno Fernandes is £12.0m but remains attractive because Manchester United face Hull in Gameweek 1 and Ipswich in Gameweek 2.
Gabriel at £8.0m is considered too expensive to combine comfortably with both Haaland and Bruno, despite Arsenal’s strong clean-sheet outlook.
Cole Palmer at £9.5m and Morgan Rogers around £7.0m-£7.5m make Chelsea a major early target, helped by no European fixtures.
Alexander Isak at £9.0m looks a standout value forward and may be preferred to Igor Thiago at £8.0m if budget allows.
Justin Kluivert is reclassified as a £7.5m midfielder, but Bournemouth’s difficult first six fixtures make him a likely wait-and-see.
Full-backs gain appeal under the bonus-point changes, boosting options such as Milos Kerkez and Pedro Porro.
FPL now includes live rank updates and official price-change predictions, with price changes moving to midnight UK time.

items.title: Ok… Who really is the goat? 🐐 🇵🇹 🇦🇷? #worldcup #ronaldo #messi
items.headline: Messi edges Ronaldo in fan GOAT debate as Pelé, Ronaldinho and R9 draw nostalgic support
items.summary:
A rapid-fire fan poll on football’s greatest player of all time leans strongly toward Lionel Messi, with many respondents naming him outright and some doing so emphatically. Cristiano Ronaldo remains the clear counterweight, receiving repeated backing from fans who see his career, longevity and goal record as enough to put him above Messi.

The debate is not limited to the modern rivalry. Pelé, Diego Maradona, Ronaldinho, Zico and Brazilian Ronaldo all receive mentions, showing a split between statistical dominance, World Cup legacy, flair and personal nostalgia. Several Brazilian icons draw support, especially from fans who value style and childhood influence as much as trophies.

There are also left-field and personal picks, including Neymar, Achraf Hakimi, Robbie Keane, Sadio Mané and Harry Maguire, but the overall pattern is clear: Messi is the most common answer, Cristiano Ronaldo is the main alternative, and the older greats remain powerful reference points in any all-time discussion.
key_points: Lionel Messi is the most frequently named GOAT in the poll.
Cristiano Ronaldo receives repeated support and is the main challenger to Messi.
Pelé, Maradona, Ronaldinho, Zico and Brazilian Ronaldo are cited as historic or nostalgic alternatives.
Brazilian Ronaldo and Ronaldinho draw notable admiration for flair and peak ability.
A few unconventional picks appear, including Neymar, Hakimi, Robbie Keane, Sadio Mané and Harry Maguire.
There is no Fantasy Premier League content, squad advice, captaincy call or transfer recommendation in the transcript.
